user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the rear of the car, including the rear bumper area, from an angle that allows assessment of surface deformation and damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a significant dent and damage to the rear bumper and trunk area of the car, matching the user's claim of new damage at the back of the car.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The first image clearly shows the front bumper of a car with visible damage including dents and scratches, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for surface marks and deformation assessment.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a light scratch on the front bumper, but the first image shows significant denting and damage inconsistent with a light scratch. The second image shows a different car with no damage, so it does not support the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The first image clearly shows the windshield with a large crack and spiderweb damage, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for visible glass damage. The second image shows a windshield with no visible damage but is less clear.
claim_status_justification: The first image clearly shows a cracked windshield consistent with the user's claim of a stone impact causing a crack spreading from the impact point. The damage is visible and matches the claim text.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the car's side mirror, which is the claimed damaged part, with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a car with a side mirror that has shattered glass, matching the user's claim that the side mirror was damaged and not sitting properly.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show parts of the car relevant to the claim, including the rear bumper and rear quarter panel, allowing for damage assessment.
claim_status_justification: The claim states the rear bumper is damaged from being tapped from behind. Image img_2 shows the rear bumper clearly with no visible dents, scratches, or damage. Image img_1 shows a dent on the rear quarter panel near the wheel, not on the bumper itself. Therefore, the visible damage does not match the claimed rear bumper damage.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a car parked on the side of a road, but the headlight is not visible or clearly shown to inspect for cracks or damage.
claim_status_justification: The image does show a car, but the claimed damaged part (headlight) is not visible or clear enough to assess any damage.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The second image clearly shows the car door panel with visible dent damage, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for assessing surface deformation.
claim_status_justification: The second image shows a clear dent on the door panel of the car, matching the user's claim of a dent along the side door panel area. The first image is too blurry to assess, but the second image provides sufficient evidence to support the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a severely damaged car with the hood open and missing, making it impossible to assess any scratches or marks on the hood surface as claimed.
claim_status_justification: The claimed damage is a scratch on the hood, but the hood is missing in the image, so the claim cannot be verified or contradicted.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop screen with visible cracks, meeting the minimum evidence requirement to inspect the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop with a cracked screen, matching the user's claim that the display glass has a crack after falling from a table.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the laptop and the hinge area damage, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for hinge damage inspection.
claim_status_justification: The images show a laptop with visible damage to the hinge area, including broken parts and exposed internal components, consistent with the claim that the hinge is broken and the screen wobbles after a drop. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop keyboard area with visible water droplets, allowing inspection for stains or damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows water droplets on the keyboard area consistent with the user's claim of water spillage and resulting stains/sticky keys. No other damage is visible, matching the claim details.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images show the laptop with clear visibility of the outer corner area, including a visible dent consistent with the claim.
claim_status_justification: The images show a laptop with a visible dent on the outer corner, matching the user's claim of damage from a fall. The dent is clearly visible in the second image with enough context to identify the laptop corner. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop screen with visible cracks, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for screen damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop with a cracked screen, matching the user's claim of screen damage that looks shattered.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image is blurry and does not clearly show any physical damage such as cracks, dents, or scratches on the trackpad area. The circled area is indistinct and does not provide clear evidence of damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop and the trackpad area but is too blurry to confirm any physical damage as claimed. No visible damage matches the claim in the provided image.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the package exterior and the claimed corner, allowing inspection of the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a package with a visibly crushed corner, matching the user's claim of corner damage upon delivery.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The first image clearly shows the package exterior and the seal side with visible torn packaging damage, meeting the minimum evidence requirement.
claim_status_justification: The first image shows a package with torn packaging on the seal side, matching the user's claim of torn seal side packaging. The second image shows an undamaged package but does not contradict the claim as it is likely a stock or reference image.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the package surface with visible wet stains and damage, meeting the minimum evidence requirement for assessing water damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a package with visible wet stains and damage consistent with water damage as claimed by the user.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 shows the inside of the package filled with crumpled paper, but the actual contents or item are not visible to verify if anything is missing. Image 2 shows the sealed exterior of the package but does not provide evidence about the contents.
claim_status_justification: The images do not clearly show the contents or item inside the package to verify if the claimed item is missing. The evidence is insufficient to support or contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the exterior of the package, including the side and top, allowing inspection for crushing damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a package with visible crushing damage on the top and side, consistent with the user's claim of a badly crushed shipping box.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the package exterior and the seal area, allowing inspection of packaging damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a package with torn packaging and tamper-evident tape that is visibly broken, matching the user's claim of a torn-open package seal. Image img_2 shows a sealed package with intact security tape, but img_1 provides clear evidence supporting the claim.
